title: malus halliana var. spontanea
intro: Malus halliana var. spontanea, also known as the Spontaneous Chinese Crabapple, is a rare and lesser-known species of apple tree native to China. It has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating sore throats, and supporting respiratory health. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and flowers of Malus halliana var. spontanea can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential applications.
